'Sole path to durable state' is currently a path to an empty room. Not a defect; the graph trees (the librarian) are its first real tenant, later.

DONE — cron cleanup + the footprint why.what: All 7 pre-Cairn (TheIgors/UU) cron jobs deleted per Akien's rule ('if it came with the OS it can stay; I have none of my own there'). The WHY, recorded in I-heartbeat-callbacks-and-bus.md + memory shrinking-footprint-event-not-poll: POLLING COSTS CPU, EVENTS DON'T; Cairn aims for an ever-DECREASING computational footprint. RULED by Akien: it traces to telos aims 1 (inference compilation) + 5 (self-improving), NOT a 7th aim or core value — aim 1 is load-bearing because Law 1 IS the footprint principle at the inference level ('don't spend compute twice'). telos.md untouched (Akien-authored).

SETTLED (this morning, earlier stretches.what: A notifier is NOT a new species — it is a SECOND CALLER of the existing Callback primitive (trigger already (now,context)->bool; body already static-by-design). What changes is who calls fires(): a GATE, event-driven, instead of only BaseShim.on_pulse. The gate POSTS THE FACT of a transition; the subscriber's shim evaluates its OWN predicate (RESOLVED by Akien — never synchronous inside the owner's write). Who holds the subscription list is the load-bearing question: the GATE holds it (owner announces, nobody reads anyone) — only that removes the polling and only that is Law 6. This DRAWS the inspector's boundary: gated -> notifier+owner acts; ungated -> inspector sweep + janitor acts (the same seam that scopes the janitor to the unowned commons).

HELD, CONVERGED, NOT CAST — the libraria.where: notes/held-librarian.json (mutable, grown across several turns); CairnCommons 534f977. Memory: librarian-graph-tree-thread.md.
HELD, CONVERGED, NOT CAST — the libraria.the_keystone: Akien's core loop ('very simple'): a query resolves by GRAPH TRAVERSAL (cheap, no inference); on a MISS the LLM is asked for THE NODES that let the graph resolve, NOT the answer; nodes fold in; the graph re-resolves. The answer always comes from structure, so re-asking is a graph walk, never a second inference. This IS Law 1 as a RUNTIME MECHANISM — inference-compilation (aim 1) and the shrinking-footprint aim are its consequences.
HELD, CONVERGED, NOT CAST — the libraria.the_settled_pieces: (1) 'The embedding IS the path' -> edges DERIVED from vector proximity, not stored (O(n) vectors); this is WHY graph trees belong in db_domain and articulated things stay git. (2) Ownership RESOLVED (device-or-function open closed): librarian OWNS the graph, embeddings generator PUTS/READS by DELEGATION (Law 6 verbatim). (3) Asymmetric indexing -> domain specialization: specialists index INTO the commons, not vice-versa unless granted (private-down default; de-instancing chokepoint is the grant-up half). (4) Self-validating at the RESOLVABILITY level (re-run confirms a path; no gap from the consumer) — TRUTH is the separate hypotheses-earn-tenure edge (reuse the probe tenure mechanism). (5) Hex = the Apple M1 inference host (concrete). (6) Ebook corpus = first layer-two seed (two-db-layers). (7) UU graph-tree embeddings generator = graft candidate (mechanism crosses like isolation.py; design re-authored).
HELD, CONVERGED, NOT CAST — the libraria.why_still_held: CONCEPT is settled; cast is blocked on SPINE work deliberately ahead of it — subscription needs inference_domain (sole path), delegation needs db_domain's FILED 'delegated access' edge (today it gates by exact owner only). Casting now would bind gates to children that can't move. HOLD is the right resting state.

open_threads: LIBRARIAN small open (my read, not ruled): when a query gap-fills, WHERE do the new nodes land? Natural answer — the querier's OWN tree (they own the query), promoted to commons only by grant. Keeps fills private-by-default, consistent with the asymmetry.
LIBRARIAN pulls db_domain's FILED 'delegated access ... later capabilities' edge — the concrete next engineering dependency once the spine reaches it. db_domain today gates by EXACT owner only.
